title: 10 best herbal capsules for indigestion
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for indigestion are dietary supplements that contain a combination of herbs known for their ability to alleviate digestive discomfort. These capsules help indigestion by promoting the production of digestive enzymes, increasing blood flow to the gastrointestinal tract, and reducing inflammation in the digestive system. As a result, herbal capsules for indigestion can effectively relieve symptoms such as bloating, gas, abdominal pain, and acid reflux. For instance, many individuals have reported experiencing significant relief from indigestion after incorporating herbal capsules into their daily routine. By taking herbal capsules, they have been able to enjoy meals without the fear of discomfort, leading to improved quality of life and greater enjoyment of food. Furthermore, herbal capsules have also been found to support overall gut health, contributing to regular bowel movements and a balanced gut microbiome.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsule helps with indigestion because it contains active compounds called gingerols and shogaols, which have been scientifically shown to promote the release of digestive enzymes in the stomach, thereby accelerating gastric emptying and alleviating symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, gas, and abdominal discomfort. Additionally, ginger possesses potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help soothe irritation in the gastrointestinal tract, further facilitating healthy digestion and easing digestive distress. Ultimately, ginger's ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and alleviate pain makes it a highly effective natural remedy for indigestion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ginger contains gingerols and shaogals which help reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, providing relief from indigestion.
Carminative: Ginger stimulates the production of digestive enzymes, helping to move food through the digestive tract and alleviating indigestion.
Antiemetic: Ginger can help reduce feelings of nausea and vomiting, common symptoms of indigestion.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of ginger help relieve pain caused by indigestion.
Spasmolytic: Ginger can help relax the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps associated with indigestion.
Antioxidant: Ginger contains powerful antioxidants that can help protect the digestive system from damage, reducing the risk of indigestion.
Antibacterial: Ginger has antibacterial properties that may help eliminate harmful bacteria in the stomach, contributing to a healthy digestive system.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Ginger rhizomes are widely used to make medicinal tea for indigestion due to their potent carminative and antispasmodic properties that aid in digestion and alleviate gastric discomfort.
Leaves: Ginger leaves are used in teas to relieve indigestion as they contain phenolic compounds, which stimulate gastric emptying and have digestive, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory effects.
Flowers: Ginger flowers can be used in herbal teas to soothe indigestion because they have a mild laxative effect that encourages gut motility and can help in managing symptoms of indigestion.
Seeds: Ginger seeds, when steeped in hot water, produce a medicinal tea that has a potential anti-inflammatory effect which helps in the reduction and prevention of inflammation-induced indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Purchase 1000 mg of ginger root powder and size 00 empty gelatin capsules.
Measure 500 mg of ginger root powder and fill each capsule halves.
Press both halves together to form a complete capsule.
Store capsules in a cool, dry place and take 1-2 capsules after meals.
Repeat usage as needed up to three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsule helps with indigestion because it contains menthol, a compound that relaxes the gastrointestinal muscles, allowing the stomach to empty more quickly and reducing the symptoms of indigestion. Additionally, peppermint oil has been shown to increase bile flow, which aids in the digestion of fats. Furthermore, it has antispasmodic properties that help to soothe and relax the intestines, providing relief from discomfort and cramping. Overall, peppermint capsule effectively alleviates indigestion due to its ability to enhance the digestion process and reduce gastrointestinal spasms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Peppermint helps relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing spasms and pain, which is important for indigestion as it can alleviate symptoms such as cramping and bloating. The active constituents responsible are menthol and methyl salicylate.

Carminative: Peppermint promotes the expulsion of gas from the digestive tract, preventing the feeling of bloating and discomfort, which is beneficial for indigestion as it can help reduce abdominal pain and improve digestion. The main active constituent responsible is menthol.

Analgesic: Peppermint has pain-relieving properties that can soothe the gastrointestinal tract, providing relief from the pain and discomfort associated with indigestion. Menthol and methyl salicylate are the active constituents responsible for this property.

Choleretic: Peppermint stimulates the production of bile, which aids in fat digestion and absorption, thus improving overall digestive function. This is important for indigestion as it can help alleviate symptoms such as feelings of fullness and heavy stomach. The active constituent responsible is menthol.

Antiemetic: Peppermint can help reduce feelings of nausea and vomiting, common in indigestion. This is helpful for indigestion as it can alleviate symptoms and improve overall comfort. The active constituent responsible is menthol.

Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating symptoms such as pain and swelling. This is beneficial for indigestion as it can help improve digestion and reduce discomfort. The active constituents responsible are menthol and rosmarinic acid.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain menthol and menthone, known to aid digestion and provide relief from indigestion symptoms.
Flowers: Rich in volatile oils, offering carminative and antispasmodic properties that help relax the gastrointestinal tract.
Seeds: Packed with antioxidants and essential oils, they promote healthy digestion and soothe upset stomachs.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1 teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves and place them in a pot, adding 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the peppermint leaves in the water for 10 minutes, then strain the liquid to remove the leaves.
Allow the peppermint tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Fill size 00 vegetarian capsules with the cooled peppermint tea using a capsule filling machine.
Store the peppermint capsules in a cool, dark place and take 1-2 capsules after meals for indigestion relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Artichoke capsule helps with indigestion because it contains cynarin, a compound that stimulates the production of bile in the liver, which in turn aids in the digestion of fats. Additionally, artichoke extract has been shown to increase gastric emptying, reduce bloating and decrease symptoms of nausea, further alleviating indigestion. The anti-inflammatory properties of artichoke also help soothe the gastrointestinal tract, providing relief from the discomfort of indigestion.
remedy_properties: Cholagogue: Artichoke capsules promote the flow of bile from the liver, which is important for indigestion as bile helps break down fats in the digestive process. The active constituents responsible for this property are cynarin and chlorogenic acid.

Carminative: Artichoke capsules help to reduce bloating and gas, common symptoms of indigestion. This is due to the artichoke's ability to increase the motility of the gastrointestinal tract, moving food through the system more smoothly. The active constituents responsible for this property are caffeoylquinic acids.

Antioxidant: Artichoke capsules have antioxidant properties that protect the digestive system from damage. This is important for indigestion as a healthy digestive system is better able to process food and absorb nutrients. The active constituents responsible for this property are cynarin, chlorogenic acid, and flavonoids like luteolin and scolymoside.

Hepatoprotective: Artichoke capsules protect the liver, which is essential for proper digestion. This is important for indigestion as a healthy liver is necessary for the production of bile and other digestive juices. The active constituents responsible for this property are cynarin and silymarin.

Antispasmodic: Artichoke capsules help to relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, which can reduce pain and discomfort associated with indigestion. The active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids like luteolin and cynarin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe the digestive tract and relieve symptoms of indigestion.
Buds: Rich in cynarin, an active compound known to stimulate bile production and facilitates digestion.
Stems: Packed with beneficial polyphenols and flavonoids that support healthy digestion and alleviate discomfort caused by indigestion.
Flowers: Contain useful bioactive compounds that aid in the breakdown of fats and proteins, thereby easing the digestion process.
remedy_recipe: Purchase 400mg artichoke capsules at your local health store.
Ingest one capsule with a full glass of water, three times daily after meals.
Consult with a healthcare professional for appropriate dosage if under 18 or pregnant.
Store capsules in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight.
Continue use for two weeks or as directed by a healthcare professional to alleviate indigestion symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsule helps with indigestion because it contains volatile oils, flavonoids, and other therapeutic substances that promote digestion and soothe gastrointestinal upset. These compounds work together to relax the smooth muscle of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing inflammation and spasms that cause discomfort. Chamomile also stimulates the production of digestive enzymes, which helps break down food more efficiently and alleviates symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating, gas, and cramping. Its calming properties can also help reduce stress-related digestive issues. Overall, chamomile capsule offers a natural and effective way to manage indigestion.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Chamomile capsules help to relieve indigestion by relaxing the smooth muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, allowing gas to pass and reducing bloating. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils such as bisabolol, matricin, and chamazulene.

Spasmolytic: Chamomile contains antispasmodic compounds that help to reduce smooth muscle spasms in the digestive tract, alleviating pain and cramping associated with indigestion. The main active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids like apigenin and luteolin.

Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile has potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, thus alleviating symptoms of indigestion. The anti-inflammatory properties are attributed to the flavonoids and terpenoids present in chamomile.

Antioxidant: Chamomile is rich in antioxidants that help protect the gastrointestinal tract from oxidative stress and damage, promoting healthy digestion. The antioxidant properties are primarily due to the presence of phenolic compounds, including flavonoids and terpenoids.

Anxiolytic: Chamomile has been shown to have anxiolytic effects, which can help reduce stress and anxiety, contributing to proper digestion. The anxiolytic properties are mainly due to the flavonoid apigenin, which binds to benzodiazepine receptors in the brain.

Antiemetic: Chamomile can help alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting associated with indigestion. The antiemetic effects are attributed to the presence of bisabolol and other volatile oils found in chamomile.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Contain oils with antispasmodic properties that relieve digestive discomfort.
Leaves: Rich in flavonoids that promote digestion and reduce inflammation in the stomach.
Seeds: Provide a source of essential oils that soothe the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating indigestion symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried chamomile flowers and place them in a heat-proof bowl.
Bring 1 cup of water to a boil, then pour it over the chamomile flowers and let them steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the chamomile liquid through a cheesecloth or fine mesh sieve into a clean container.
Fill size "00" empty gelatin capsules with the chamomile liquid using a capsule filling machine.
Allow the filled capsules to dry at room temperature for at least 24 hours before storing them in a tightly sealed container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsule helps with indigestion because it contains active compounds that promote digestive health. The key component, glycyrrhizin, encourages the production of mucus in the gastrointestinal tract, creating a protective barrier against stomach acid. This enables the stomach to function optimally, allowing for efficient digestion of food and reducing instances of indigestion. Additionally, licorice has been shown to stimulate the secretion of digestive enzymes, further facilitating the breakdown of food in the stomach. Consequently, incorporating licorice capsules into a daily routine can offer relief and prevent discomfort associated with indigestion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is important for indigestion as it can help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.

Mucosal protective: Licorice can form a protective mucosal barrier in the stomach, which is important for indigestion as it can help prevent damage to the stomach lining caused by acid. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin and its derivatives.

Antacid: Licorice can neutralize stomach acid, which is important for indigestion as it can help relieve symptoms such as heartburn and acid reflux. The active constituents responsible for this property are saponins and flavonoids.

Prokinetic: Licorice can stimulate the movement of the gastrointestinal tract, which is important for indigestion as it can help speed up digestion and reduce symptoms such as bloating and fullness. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin and its metabolites.

Antispasmodic: Licorice can relax the smooth muscle in the gastrointestinal tract, which is important for indigestion as it can help reduce cramping and spasms. The active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids and triterpenoids.

Antimicrobial: Licorice can inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the digestive tract, which is important for indigestion as it can help prevent infections and maintain a healthy gut microbiome. The active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids and triterpenoids.

Anti-ulcer: Licorice can promote the healing of ulcers in the digestive tract, which is important for indigestion as it can help treat and prevent the formation of ulcers caused by excessive stomach acid. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin and its metabolites.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that aids in digestion.
Rhizomes: Rich in flavonoids and coumarins, promoting digestive health by soothing gut inflammation.
Leaves: Source of various antioxidant compounds, assisting in reducing digestive oxidative stress.
Flowers: Contain essential oils and polyphenols, known to alleviate indigestion and promote gut motility.
remedy_recipe: Combine 2 teaspoons of licorice root powder, 1 teaspoon of slippery elm bark powder, and 1/2 teaspoon of fennel seeds in a mixing bowl.
Stir in just enough water to form a thick, pliable dough, allowing 5-10 minutes for absorption.
Divide the dough into 12 equal parts, rolling each into a small capsule shape with your hands.
Let the licorice capsules dry at room temperature for 24 hours, turning them occasionally for even drying.
Store the dried licorice capsules in an airtight container for up to 2 weeks, taking 1-2 capsules after meals as necessary for indigestion relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsule helps with indigestion because it contains curcumin, a bioactive compound that stimulates the production of bile in the stomach, which is necessary for fat digestion. Moreover, curcumin helps to reduce inflammation in the gut, improving gut motility and alleviating symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, gas, and stomach cramps. Additionally, turmeric has antioxidant properties that protect the gut lining from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Curcumin, the main active ingredient in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating indigestion symptoms.
Antioxidant: Turmeric's high antioxidant content helps protect the gut lining from free radical damage, which can contribute to indigestion.
Carminative: Turmeric can help reduce gas and bloating in the digestive tract, making it easier to digest food.
Cholagogue: Turmeric stimulates the production of bile in the liver, which aids in the digestion of fats and can help prevent indigestion.
Spasmolytic: Turmeric can help relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and cramping associated with indigestion.
Bactericidal: Curcumin has been shown to have antibacterial properties, which can help kill harmful bacteria in the gut that can cause indigestion.
Immunomodulatory: Turmeric can help regulate the immune system, which can help reduce inflammation and improve digestion.
Hepatoprotective: Turmeric can protect the liver from damage, which is important for overall digestive health.
Choleretic: Turmeric stimulates the flow of bile from the liver, which aids in the digestion of fats and can help prevent indigestion.
Antiemetic: Turmeric can help reduce nausea and vomiting, common symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric's underground stem or rhizome contains curcumin, a compound known to aid in digestion by stimulating the production of bile in the liver.
Roots: The fleshy roots of turmeric have a similar composition to the rhizomes and are used interchangeably, providing soothing relief for indigestion.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, turmeric leaves contribute to reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating indigestion symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Purchase organic turmeric powder and size 00 gelatin capsules, providing 500mg of turmeric per capsule.
Measure and mix 1/2 teaspoon of turmeric powder with 1 tablespoon of organic olive oil.
Fill each gelatin capsule with the turmeric-oil mixture using a capsule filling machine or tweezers.
Allow the filled capsules to dry for 24 hours at room temperature.
Store the dried turmeric capsules in an airtight container for up to 3 months, taking 1-2 capsules after each meal to alleviate indigestion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsule helps with indigestion because it contains volatile oils, such as fenchone, estragole, and anethole, which have antispasmodic properties. These compounds help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing symptoms of bloating, gas, and cramps. Furthermore, fennel has carminative effects, which means it helps eliminate gas from the digestive tract, alleviating discomfort and pain. Its high fiber content also promotes regular bowel movements and supports overall digestive health. Consequently, fennel capsules can effectively manage indigestion and associated symptoms, providing quick relief and improving the quality of life.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Fennel helps to expel intestinal gas and alleviate symptoms of bloating and cramping, which are common symptoms of indigestion. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils such as anethole, fenchone, and estragole.

Spasmolytic: Fennel can help to relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and promoting normal digestion. This property is attributed to the plant's volatile oils and rosmarinic acid.

Antioxidant: Fennel contains antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, that can help protect the gastrointestinal tract from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to indigestion.

Choleretic: Fennel can help stimulate the production of bile in the liver, which is important for the digestion and absorption of fats. This property can help improve overall digestive function and reduce symptoms of indigestion.

Anti-inflammatory: Fennel has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract and alleviate symptoms of indigestion. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils and flavonoids.
Antiemetic: Fennel can help to reduce nausea and vomiting, which can be symptoms of indigestion. This property is attributed to the plant's essential oils and terpene compounds.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds contain oils that stimulate the production of gastric juices, aiding in digestion.
Leaves: Fennel leaves have carminative properties that help relieve symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating and gas.
Rhyzomes: Fennel rhyzomes contain antispasmodic compounds that soothe the digestive tract and alleviate discomfort caused by indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Purchase 1 tablespoon of fennel seeds and a capsule-making machine, ensuring the seeds are fresh and the machine is clean.
Grind the fennel seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, requiring 5-10 minutes.
Measure 500mg of the fennel powder for each capsule, filling the machine's designated area.
Press the machine's plunger until the capsule is sealed, separating the halves.
Store the fennel capsules in a cool, dark place for up to six months, consuming 1-2 capsules after meals for indigestion relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsule helps with indigestion because it promotes the production of digestive juices in the stomach. These juices include bile, stomach acid, and enzymes that are essential for breaking down food particles, thus improving nutrient absorption and reducing the discomfort caused by indigestion. Moreover, the fiber content in dandelion aids in regulating bowel movements, alleviating symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, constipation, and gas. Furthermore, dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties can soothe the gastrointestinal tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that may contribute to indigestion.
remedy_properties: Choleretic property: Dandelion capsules stimulate the production of bile in the liver, which is important for indigestion as it aids in the breakdown and absorption of fats and nutrients. The active constituents responsible for this property are taraxacin, taraxacerin, and taraxasterol.

Diuretic property: Dandelion capsules promote urination, which helps remove waste and toxins from the body. This is important for indigestion as it can help reduce bloating and water retention in the gastrointestinal tract, thus alleviating discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property are potassium, sodium, and sesquiterpene lactones.

Carminative property: Dandelion capsules help reduce gas and bloating in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of indigestion. This property is important for indigestion as it can help reduce pain and discomfort in the abdomen. The active constituents responsible for this property are volatile oils, such as sesquiterpene lactones.

Antioxidant property: Dandelion capsules contain antioxidants that help protect the digestive system from damage caused by free radicals. This property is important for indigestion as it can help reduce inflammation and promote overall digestive health. The active constituents responsible for this property include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and terpenoids.

Antibacterial property: Dandelion capsules have antibacterial properties that can help prevent the growth of harmful bacteria in the digestive tract. This property is important for indigestion as it can help prevent infections and maintain a healthy balance of bacteria in the gut. The active constituents responsible for this property include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and terpenoids.

Bitter property: Dandelion capsules have a bitter taste that can stimulate the production of digestive enzymes and bile, which can help improve digestion and absorption of nutrients. This property is important for indigestion as it can help alleviate symptoms such as bloating, gas, and abdominal discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property include sesquiterpene lactones, taraxacoside, and taraxacerin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain high amounts of bitter compounds that stimulate digestive juices.
Leaves: Rich in vitamins and minerals that promote overall digestive health.
Flowers: Contain bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.
Buds: High in antioxidants that protect the stomach lining and support digestive function.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of fresh dandelion flowers, ensuring petals are completely dry and free of debris.
Submerge flowers in 4 cups of water, bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain liquid, keeping only the liquid which should measure about 3 cups.
Pour liquid into a glass container, seal and store in refrigerator for 24 hours to allow sediment to settle.
Carefully pour off clear liquid into a separate container, discarding sediment, then fill gelatin capsules with the liquid and seal. Dosage is 1 capsule after each meal.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle capsule helps with indigestion because it contains a powerful antioxidant called silymarin, which supports healthy digestion by protecting the liver and promoting the production of bile. This, in turn, allows the body to break down and absorb nutrients from food more efficiently, reducing the symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, gas, and discomfort. Additionally, milk thistle has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and heal the digestive tract, further aiding in the digestion process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Milk thistle capsules contain silymarin, which helps reduce inflammation in the gut, providing relief from indigestion symptoms such as bloating, pain, and discomfort.

Antioxidant: The flavonoid luteolin found in milk thistle has antioxidant properties, protecting the digestive tract from damage caused by free radicals, and reducing inflammation associated with indigestion.
Protects liver function: Milk thistle extract aids in regenerating liver cells and protecting the liver from toxins, promoting overall digestive health and reducing symptoms of indigestion.
Promotes bile production: Milk thistle helps stimulate the production of bile in the liver, improving the digestion of fats and reducing the risk of indigestion and associated symptoms.
Reduces spasms: Milk thistle has a muscle-relaxing effect, which helps reduce spasms in the digestive tract, providing relief from cramps and discomfort caused by indigestion.
Supports gut microbiome: The antimicrobial properties of milk thistle help maintain a healthy balance of gut bacteria, improving digestion and reducing symptoms of indigestion.
Regenerates gut lining: Milk thistle promotes the regeneration of gut lining, reducing the risk of leaky gut syndrome, and improving overall digestive health, thus alleviating indigestion.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Contain silymarin, a flavonoid that aids in digestion by promoting the flow of bile from the liver.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe the digestive tract and ease indigestion.
Flowers: Contain bioactive compounds that support healthy digestion and help alleviate symptoms of indigestion.
Rhyzomes: Underground stems that store nutrients, including antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which aid in digestion.
remedy_recipe: Purchase 200-400mg milk thistle extract capsules from a reputable health store.
Ingest 1-2 capsules daily, preferably with meals, to aid in digestion.
Consult a healthcare professional before taking milk thistle if pregnant, breastfeeding, or taking medication.
Store milk thistle capsules in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight.
Discard capsules if expiration date has passed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsule helps with indigestion because it contains a substance called mucilage, which coats the stomach and intestinal lining, protecting it from excess acid and irritation. This action soothes the digestive tract, reducing discomfort and promoting healthy digestion. Additionally, marshmallow has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, further aiding in the relief of indigestion symptoms. Overall, marshmallow capsule offers a natural and effective solution for improving digestive health and function.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Marshmallow capsules have a soothing effect on the mucous membranes of the gastrointestinal tract, helping to protect and relieve inflammation in the digestive system. This property is important for indigestion as it helps reduce irritation and discomfort in the stomach and intestines. The active constituents responsible for this property are primarily mucilage polysaccharides found in the marshmallow plant.

Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow capsules contain compounds with anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. This property is important for indigestion as it alleviates pain and irritation caused by inflammation in the stomach and intestines. The active constituents contributing to this property include flavonoids, such as quercetin and luteolin.

Mild laxative: Marshmallow capsules can help promote regular bowel movements by mildly stimulating the gastrointestinal tract, making it easier to pass stools. This property is important for indigestion as it can help relieve symptoms of constipation and bloating. The active constituents responsible for this property are the mucilage polysaccharides and small amounts of fiber found in marshmallow.

Emollient: Marshmallow capsules can help soothe and soften the mucous membranes, providing a protective barrier against irritants and toxins. This property is important for indigestion as it helps shield the digestive tract from substances that can cause further irritation and discomfort. The active constituents contributing to this property are the mucilage polysaccharides.

Digestive aid: Marshmallow capsules can help improve overall digestion by increasing the secretion of digestive enzymes and promoting the motility of the gastrointestinal tract. This property is important for indigestion as it enhances the breakdown and absorption of nutrients, reducing the likelihood of symptoms associated with poor digestion. The active constituents responsible for this property are the mucilage polysaccharides and certain flavonoids found in marshmallow.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high amounts of tannins and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe the digestive tract and relieve indigestion.
Flowers: Rich in mucilage, which coats the stomach lining and protects it from irritation, making it effective in treating indigestion.
Seeds: High in fiber, which aids in digestion, prevents constipation, and alleviates symptoms of indigestion.
Roots: Contain high levels of ascorbic acid, which helps in the breakdown of food, thus improving digestion.
Stems: Rich in antioxidants and contain compounds that can stimulate the digestive system, making it effective for treating indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Combine 1 tablespoon of unflavored gelatin and 1/4 cup of cold water in a small bowl, letting it sit for 5 minutes to soften.

Heat 1/2 cup of water, 1 cup of sugar, and 1/2 cup of light corn syrup in a saucepan over medium heat, stirring until the sugar dissolves, then bring to a boil and cook for 8-10 minutes without stirring until the mixture reaches 240 degrees Fahrenheit.
Pour the hot syrup over the gelatin mixture, beating on low speed for 2-3 minutes, then increase the speed to high and beat for 10-15 minutes until the mixture is thick, white, and fluffy.
Add 1 teaspoon of vanilla extract and 1/2 teaspoon of peppermint oil, beating for an additional minute until well combined.

Transfer the marshmallow mixture to a piping bag or plastic bag with a corner snipped off, and pipe it into 1-inch round shapes onto a baking sheet lined with parchment paper; let the capsules dry for at least 2 hours before storing in an airtight container for up to 2 weeks.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat indigestion with herbal capsules is by using specified herbs with documented properties to alleviate digestive discomfort. For example, capsules containing ginger, fennel, or peppermint extracts can effectively reduce indigestion symptoms due to their carminative and antispasmodic effects. Artichoke extract capsules can also be beneficial as they stimulate bile production, promoting healthy digestion and absorption. Always consult a healthcare professional before starting any new supplement regimen.
supplementary_causes: The primary causes of indigestion that are treatable with herbal capsules are poor dietary habits, stress, and lack of digestive enzymes. These factors can contribute to indigestion by slowing down the digestive process, causing bloating, gas, and discomfort. Herbal capsules can help address these issues by providing natural anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties that soothe the digestive tract, stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, and promote regular bowel movements. Additionally, some herbal capsules can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can also contribute to indigestion.
supplementary_preparations: Other herbal preparations to use with capsules for indigestion are teas, tinctures, and essential oils. These can provide targeted relief by increasing digestive enzyme production, reducing inflammation, and soothing upset stomachs. Additionally, enteric-coated capsules can be complemented with topical applications like abdominal massages using infused oils or heating pads filled with herbal mixtures, promoting circulation and alleviating discomfort.
supplementary_frequency: For indigestion, you should drink herbal capsules every six to eight hours, up to three times a day. This regimen ensures a consistent level of the herbal remedy in your system to support digestion, while avoiding an overload that could lead to unnecessary side effects or complications. Taking the capsules too frequently or in larger amounts than recommended does not necessarily improve their effectiveness and may cause adverse reactions.
supplementary_side_effects: The possible side effects associated with consuming herbal capsules for indigestion include stomach discomfort, diarrhea, and allergic reactions. In some cases, they might lead to interactions with other medications, affecting their efficacy or causing adverse effects. Pregnant or breastfeeding women, individuals with medical conditions, and those taking prescribed drugs should consult a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies to manage indigestion.
